zh.St. Mary's Fenchurch, London, England: 英國英格蘭倫敦易阿林聖 馬也斯
zh.lifeSummary: Sir Reginald atte Conduit於大約1266年出生。他於大約1290年與Mrs. Reginald atte Conduit在英國英格蘭倫敦結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1347年12月6日在英國英格蘭倫敦易阿林聖 馬也斯享年82歲。

zh-hans.St. Mary's Fenchurch, London, England: 英国英格兰倫敦易阿林聖 馬也斯

nameMeanings.name: atte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Cecile, Gervais, Jean Pierre, Jean-Claude, Leonce, Pierre.
South German: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow, from Middle High German matte ‘meadow’.
German: from a short form of the personal name Matthias or Matthäus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Matter
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived by a meadow, from Matte 1 + -er, suffix denoting an inhabitant.
English (Lancashire); Dutch (also De Matter): occupational name for a maker of mats, from an agent derivative of Middle English matte, Middle Dutch mat ‘mat’.

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

nameMeanings.name: Conduit
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Middle English conduit, condit, cundit, originally an artificial channel or pipe for conveying water, later a fountain or pump. The surname probably refers to the latter. Condict and Cundict are hypercorrect pronunciations of Condit and Cundit, subsequently altered to Conduct by folk etymology. The surname may sometimes have been confused with Cundy .

variants.name: Condict
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brigid, Fergus, Liam.

English (Cornwall): hypercorrected form of Condit(t) and Cundit(t), from Middle English conduit, condit, cundit, originally an artificial channel or pipe for conveying water, later a fountain or pump (the surname probably refers to the latter). The surname may sometimes have been confused with Cundy . See also Condit .
Irish: variant of Connick , a patronymic from Gaelic Mac Conmhaic.

variants.name: Condick
meanings: English:  see  Conduit   and  compare  Conduct  .

variants.name: Cundict
meanings: English:  see  Conduit   and  compare  Conduct  .

variants.name: Cundy
meanings: English (Cornwall and Devon, of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy and Picardy called Condé, a French form of the Gaulish condate ‘junction of rivers’. Members of the de Condé family held land in Lincolnshire, Kent, Leicestershire, Cambridgeshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th century. The strong presence of the surname in 16th-century Cornwall and Devon is unexpected, but is probably the result of migration from counties further east or north. Derivation from the Cornish language is implausible, and there is no known place in Cornwall which could have given rise to it as a habitational name.

variants.name: Cundick
meanings: English:  see  Conduit   and  compare  Conduct  .

variants.name: Conduct
meanings: English:  see  Conduit   and  compare  Condick  .

nameMeanings.name: Reginald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Norman origin, derived from Reginaldus, a Latinized form of Reynold influenced by Latin regina ‘queen’. The full form is now regarded as very formal, and bearers generally shorten it to Reg in ordinary usage.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Reginald atte Conduit was born about 1266. He married Mrs. Reginald atte Conduit about 1290, in London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 6 December 1347, in St Mary's, Ealing, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 82.

Il est décédé le 6 décembre 1347, à St Mary's, Ealing, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 82 ans.

Er starb am 6. Dezember 1347 im Alter von 82 Jahren in St Mary's, Ealing, London, England, United Kingdom.

ko.St. Mary's Fenchurch, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 올잉 스트 마이'스
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Reginald atte Conduit(은)는 대략 1266년 태어났다. 그는 대략 1290년 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 Mrs. Reginald atte Conduit(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1347년 12월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 올잉 스트 마이'스에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.
